item.title: Excerpts, April 2026
item.description:
The excerpts are from books I hereby recommend to the reader of my blog, and they give you a peek at what you will be getting yourself into.

These are not meant to be excerpts of all the books I have read in the past few months that I find to be well written. Such a post would be so long that we would end up with a thick book filled with excerpts: that time would be better spent reading one of the books mentioned here in full. That is the intention after all, viz. to give you recommendations with spoilers. Hence, there will only be very few recommendations such that you can add them to your list of things to read for the near future. If you want more recommendations, ask me personally.

If your RSS reader didn't make it obvious where to read the full article. Here it is: https://lewoof.xyz/posts/excerpts-24-04-2026.html

item.title: Addresses updated
item.description:
Here goes another post that is not posted on the site. I reset my Ledger wallet today and therefore have a new Solana address and a new Bitcoin address. I had to remove the Monero addresses from my pages as I did not install Monero again on the wallet, and do not plan to do so until I host my own node. The new addresses are listed below.

  • Solana: GHHPYZDDyXmwPLnQPEBgXAD9FzZmDPhCbVRpgqxuEpQ3
  • Bitcoin (native segwit): bc1qqzqw40g566rmyvafrccjagktmd9nqvyqwcz6jh

Anything sent to my old wallets after Apr 22 2026 14:50 IST (UTC+0530) will be lost. If you want to make a donation, please use the new addresses, my BuyMeACoffee, or walk up to me and hand me cash (the most secure and private way to do it at the moment).

Have a good day.

item.title: Woof OS Update
item.description:
The notice is on the Woof OS website instead of the posts on www.lewoof.xyz. Below is the notice:

I no longer use Arch Linux. I have switched to Void. Woof OS therefore will follow suit and will eventually be a respin of Void Linux instead. I am very busy however so this will not happen for at least a year. Woof OS installs will work and update just fine since they are just respins of Arch Linux, but the ISOs will not be updated. When Woof OS does get updated, there will be a post on my blog (lewoof.xyz). Add the RSS feed, https://lewoof.xyz/posts/feed.xml to your reader to be notified when there is a new post. I'm too lazy to make a separate RSS feed for this (temporarily) dead project.

item.title: A little update for my RSS users
item.description:
I now have an RSS feed for personal summaries I've written of courses I take. They were originally meant as something for me to be able to share with friends who are on the same course as me, and for me to revisit concepts that I often forget. However, I have realised that the reason I had to write them myself was that I could not find any concise notes online of these courses, and the ones that did exist were outdated, not concise, not accurate, or do not properly cover the syllabus in its entirety. I hope that these notes are useful for anyone taking these courses, or are interested in the topics and would like something that they can have printed out to look at everyday in order to recall ideas from said topics.

You can add the notes feed with this feed URL: https://lewoof.xyz/notes/feed.xml

It could also be added by sharing my site's homepage to your RSS reader which might (depending on its features) detect the blog post feed and the notes feed.

This post will not be posted on the site as a page. This is also not a proper blog post. Rather it is similar to a broadcast text message. It is an exclusive for you (not very exclusive considering all it takes is an RSS reader to see this, but one is not to expect such unicorns in the slopocalypse that is 2026), the few people who follow the feed. Other posts (mostly) however will be on the site as HTML pages as well as RSS feed items.

item.title: Using PGP
item.description: Arguing that you don’t care about the right to privacy because you have nothing to hide is no different from saying you don’t care about free speech because you have nothing to say. – Edward Snowden
